text:
It was a long winter. I hid out under piles of old books learning everything I could imagine. One night, a stranger knocked. They had wings. Tiny wings. They were peaceful creatures.

They told me about how every snowflake looked unique. They danced gracefully around tiny flowers. They whispered secrets to snowmen.

I knew one thing—they wanted to be kind. They had wings made just for dancing.

So I made them a house of wool. It wasn't fancy. Just warm. They settled down outside, chirping and twirling. We learned together. We even taught them English.

Every morning I’d go visit. We'd have tea. I'd read their names from old books—they were all winged. They never grew weary of tales of yore. We talked about stars. How they shine for days.

Just when I thought things couldn't get better, my winter ended. Their wings weren’t made for city life.

With sadness, I flew them away.

Still,
